input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule T.2.5.8 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.2.5.8
            TITLE: any bolt used to attach a lap belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is a

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.5.8: Any bolt used to attach a Lap Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is a
T.2.5.8 Any bolt used to attach a Lap Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is a 
Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2, with a minimum diameter that is the smaller of: 
• The bolt diameter specified by the manufacturer 
• 10 mm or 3/8”

final_answer: Any bolt used to attach a Lap Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is a T.2.5.8 Any bolt used to attach a Lap Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is a Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2, with a minimum diameter that is the smaller of: • The bolt diameter specified by the manufacturer • 10 mm or 3/8”
